title: Why has Max Verstappen lost Gianpiero Lambiase in shock McLaren move?
summary:
In a shock move, Max Verstappen’s race engineer Gianpiero Lambiase is set to leave Red Bull to join McLaren in 2028. The long-term plan is for Lambiase, who has worked with Verstappen since May 2016, to replace Andrea Stella as McLaren team principal.

A merry-go-round is on the cards, with Stella reportedly high on Ferrari’s wishlist to take over from Frédéric Vasseur in the future. For Red Bull, the exit of Lambiase at the end of 2027 represents the departure of yet another major figure from the Milton Keynes-based team.

title: Finally F1 cars don’t need to be faster to overtake – Isack Hadjar
summary:
Red Bull driver Isack Hadjar says Formula 1 overtakes are now possible with similar pace, while they previously required the attacking car to be much faster.
F1’s new power unit era features more electrical power and therefore a much greater emphasis on energy management.
In turn, energy management means drivers get to overtake much more easily as they slow down earlier before corners, and ...Keep reading

title: Question of the week: What, if anything, does F1 need to change in the current rules?

Change, as always, stirs debate – and while Formula 1 has been through many regulatory shifts before, the scale of reaction in 2026 has been notably different.

The Briton therefore avenged his near-miss in 1994 to take what would be his only F1 title and 30 years on, Hill has returned to his old stable as an ambassador.
